categories.name: Enduring the Cold
categories.description: There's worse things than being on a tropical island.

questions.question: Survivalists often cite 'The Rules of 3' for extreme conditions like freezing cold. What are they?

answers.text: walk 3 miles in any any direction, find 3 meals, yell 3 times, climb 3 trees
dig three feet underground, 3 feet of wood or rocks on top, 3 fires to stay extra warm
3 minutes without air or in icy water, 3 hours without shelter, 3 days without water, 3 weeks without food

questions.question: Thomas Musgrave, Fellow of the Royal Geographical Society, was shipwrecked on the subantarctic Auckland island with his crew of five in 1864, how did they survive the cold?

answers.text: Discovering a large cave on the island and building a windproof door out of seal skins.
Burning the ship piece by piece to keep a fire going.
By disassembling their ship and building a cabin out of it.
By building a network of igloos that also worked as a seal trap.

categories.name: Pop Culture Foraging
categories.description: Persistence in Movies, TV, music, and the internet.

questions.question: Robinson Curusoe is based on the real person, Alexander Selkirk who survived alone on an island for four years and four months in the early 1700's. How did he get stranded there?

answers.text: He survived a pirate attack and landed there as the sole survivor.
He requested to remain on the island after deciding their vessel was not seaworthy.
He was marooned by the Spanish Navy for assisting smugglers.
He escaped a mutiny after his crew decided they could find Atlantis.

questions.factoid: He begged to be let back on board as they were leaving, but the ship actually did sink a short time later.

questions.question: Which logo is shown here?

answers.text: Apple Music
Spotify
Pandora
Tidal

questions.factoid: Spotify launched in Sweden in 2008 and now has over 600 million users worldwide.

categories.name: DIY Rx Maintenance
categories.description: No doctors? No dentists? No paperwork?

questions.question: What's the best way to treat a bad tooth if you're deserted on an island without a dentist?

answers.text: Leave the tooth in, saltwater rinse, keep clean, only pull with a cloth if truly loose.
Lay with the tooth submerged in water, ocean creatures will clean the infected area.
Create a rudimentary lathe from coconuts to drill out the infected tooth.
Fashion crowns from carefully selected seashells and seal with coconut resin.

questions.factoid: Most forms of pulling or breaking teeth are able to lead to deep, life-threatening infections. Consider booking ocean flights going to dental conferences.

questions.question: In the comic stip Calvin and Hobbes, what was the occupation of Calvin's father?

answers.text: An Accountant
A Phlebotomist
A Binocular Company CEO
A Patent Lawyer

questions.factoid: This is revealed in only one strip where his father reads him the patent information at his desk as a sardonic 'story' over the phone.

categories.name: Ceramic Duck Hunt
categories.description: One special question to claim custodianship of the duck regardless of what team wins. The person that correctly guesses gets the duck. Also grants their team points.

questions.question: Traditional ceramics are not considered agglomerate materials, but they heavily rely on what process to lock their individual particles together?

answers.text: sintering
fission
chemical binding
smelting
